title: [Goal] Flow Switcher button in CoreUIBar
description:
## Description

When viewing a prototype, there's no easy way to see which flow is active or switch between available flows. Users currently need to manually change the `?flow=` URL param or navigate via the Viewfinder. This adds friction, especially for prototypes with many flows (e.g., Signup has 4 flows: empty-form, validation-errors, prefilled-review, error-state).

Add a Flow Switcher button to the CoreUIBar (the floating bottom-right chrome bar). The button detects the current prototype from the URL pathname, lists all flows scoped to that prototype using `listFlows()`, shows the active flow name, and opens a dropdown menu to switch between them via `resolveFlowRoute()`.

The switcher is a Svelte component in `packages/core/src/`, config-driven via `core-ui.config.json`, and hidden when the current prototype has ≤1 flow. It uses core-only data APIs (no React dependency) and follows the same DropdownMenu pattern as CreateMenuButton and CommentsMenuButton.

A `useFlows()` React hook will also be exported from `@dfosco/storyboard-react` to expose flow switching to page-level components.

## Tasks

- [x] **#g017#t01**: Create FlowSwitcherButton.svelte — dropdown menu listing flows for the current prototype with active indicator
- [x] **#g017#t02**: Add flows menu entry to core-ui.config.json with icon, modes, and ariaLabel
- [x] **#g017#t03**: Wire FlowSwitcherButton into CoreUIBar — dynamic import, show only when prototype has >1 flow
- [x] **#g017#t04**: Add useFlows() hook to @dfosco/storyboard-react — returns flows, activeFlow, switchFlow for current prototype
- [x] **#g017#t05**: Unit tests for flow filtering logic — prototype scoping, single flow hiding, global flow exclusion
- [x] **#g017#t06**: Refactored flow switcher to use action registry pattern — config declares action: core/flows, generic ActionMenuButton renders items
- [x] **#g017#t07**: Fix missing canvasEntry snippet in Viewfinder
  Added the missing #snippet canvasEntry(canvas) in Viewfinder.svelte. The canvas view mode was crashing with ReferenceError because canvasEntry was referenced but never defined.
- [x] **#g017#t08**: Fix duplicate folder rendering in Viewfinder prototype view
  Removed stale sortedFolders loop that rendered all folders unconditionally outside the viewMode check, causing folders to appear twice in prototype view.

---
_Managed by clips • Goal ID: #g017_
